text: The surgeon uses robotic instruments to mobilize the liver, separating it from surrounding scar tissue and attachments. The robotic tools provide enhanced articulation, allowing precise manipulation of the liver's connections to the area behind it.

The surgeon uses an intraoperative ultrasound device to visualize the tumor within the liver, scanning for additional lesions. A harmonic scalpel is employed to score and coagulate a line on the liver surface, marking the area for resection. The scalpel divides the liver tissue, ensuring a margin of healthy liver is maintained around the tumor for complete removal.

The surgeon controls robotic instruments to perform coagulation through the liver, minimizing blood loss and providing gentle retraction. An assistant uses a suction aspirator to manage fluids. The robotic system offers enhanced three-dimensional visualization, allowing precise manipulation and vessel grasping if bleeding occurs.

The surgeon rotates the liver to access and dissect along its posterior border. Clips are visible on the gallbladder structures following its removal, as it was obstructing the resection margin. The dissection continues through the liver to remove the specimen, followed by coagulation to cauterize the liver margin and prevent oozing. A hemostatic agent is occasionally applied to the liver surface.
